title: Athyriopsis × lobatocrenata
taxonomy: Athyriopsis × lobatocrenata is a hybrid species within the Athyriaceae family, which is a group of ferns commonly known as lady ferns. This hybrid species is the result of a cross between two different species of ferns. The classification of Athyriopsis × lobatocrenata is still a matter of ongoing research, but it is generally accepted to be a member of the Pteridophyta division. As a hybrid species, its classification is often debated among botanists and may be subject to change as more information becomes available. Further study is needed to fully understand the taxonomic classification of Athyriopsis × lobatocrenata.

intro: Athyriopsis × lobatocrenata, also known as the Japanese lady fern, is a hybrid species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, and the modulation of immune responses. The bioactive constituents of Athyriopsis × lobatocrenata include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, this plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infusions and decoctions to treat various health conditions.
